notes: Week-101: Reputation Score MVP, dashboard data refresh, CTO tech debt, invoice PDF export, agent leaderboard, health monitoring. Sprints 138-154 all confirmed done on server (week-99 + auto-sprints). These are the next highest-value product tasks.

tasks.description: Reactivate Manus CMO — re-enable wallet, start PM2 processes, smoke test
tasks.context:
The CMO was deliberately deactivated on 2026-03-13 (CTO-20260222-002). Owner has confirmed reactivation.

PREREQUISITE: MANUS_API_KEY is confirmed present in /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/.env — proceed directly.

STEP 1 — Re-enable CMO wallet in Supabase:
Run on server:
  cd /home/invoica/apps/Invoica
  node -e "
    const { PrismaClient } = require('@prisma/client');
    const prisma = new PrismaClient();
    prisma.agentWallet.updateMany({ where: { agentId: 'cmo' }, data: { isActive: true } })
      .then(r => { console.log('updated:', r.count, 'rows'); prisma.$disconnect(); })
      .catch(e => { console.error(e); prisma.$disconnect(); });
  "
If no wallet table exists: skip this step and note it in the report.

STEP 2 — Clear stale cron guards:
  rm -f /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/logs/cron-guard-cmo-daily-watch.json

STEP 3 — Start CMO PM2 processes:
  cd /home/invoica/apps/Invoica
  pm2 start ecosystem.config.js --only cmo-daily-watch,cmo-weekly-content-plan
  pm2 save
  pm2 status

STEP 4 — Smoke test:
  pm2 restart cmo-daily-watch
  sleep 10 && pm2 logs cmo-daily-watch --lines 30 --nostream
  Check for: Manus task created, polling, completed — OR any API error

STEP 5 — Write report to: reports/cto/cmo-reactivation-2026-03-19.md
  Include: wallet update result, PM2 status, smoke test output, any errors.

tasks.description: Build Reputation Score MVP — GET /v1/reputation/:agentId returns computed reputation score based on payment history
Update dashboard frontend to consume new analytics endpoints — peak hours, invoice aging, payment lag, growth metrics
tasks.context:
The swarm has built 17 new analytics endpoints (sprints 138-154) but the dashboard frontend still shows basic stats from the original launch. This task connects the dashboard to the new data.

NEW ENDPOINTS TO WIRE (confirmed built in sprints 143-154):
  - GET /v1/metrics/peak-hours → { hour, count }[] — 24hr bar chart
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/aging → aging buckets (0-7d, 7-30d, 30d+)
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/payment-lag → { medianDays, avgDays, p90Days }
  - GET /v1/metrics/growth → { week, count, amount }[] weekly growth
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/void → void rate stats

TARGET FILE: frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx (or wherever the main dashboard is)

BEFORE WRITING:
  1. Read frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx to understand current structure
  2. Read frontend/lib/api-client.ts to see existing API call patterns

ADD to api-client.ts (after existing fetch functions):
  - fetchPeakHours(): Promise<{ hour: number; count: number }[]>
  - fetchInvoiceAging(): Promise<{ bucket: string; count: number; totalAmount: number }[]>
  - fetchPaymentLag(): Promise<{ medianDays: number; avgDays: number; p90Days: number }>
  - fetchGrowthMetrics(): Promise<{ week: string; count: number; amount: number }[]>

ADD to dashboard page — 2 new stat cards (keep it minimal, don't redesign):
  Card 1 — 'Payment Speed': show medianDays from payment-lag (e.g. 'Avg 2.4 days to settle')
  Card 2 — 'Peak Hour': show top hour from peak-hours (e.g. 'Peak: 14:00 UTC')

Do NOT add charts — just stat cards. Max 60 new lines across both files.
After: run npx tsc --noEmit in frontend/ dir.

tasks.description: Split large frontend files — api-keys page and any other files over 300 lines (CTO-20260215-006)
tasks.context:
CTO flagged: frontend files are growing beyond the 200-line one-file-per-call architecture limit. Priority target: api-keys page.

STEP 1 — Find large files:
  Run: find frontend/app -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' | xargs wc -l | sort -rn | head -10
  List files over 250 lines.

STEP 2 — For api-keys page (frontend/app/api-keys/page.tsx or similar):
  Read the file. Split into:
  - page.tsx (the page component — data fetching + layout, max 100 lines)
  - components/ApiKeyList.tsx (the table/list component)
  - components/CreateApiKeyModal.tsx (the creation form/modal if it exists)
  Keep all existing functionality — this is purely structural refactoring.

STEP 3 — Check if other dashboard pages exceed 300 lines and apply same split pattern.

RULE: Do NOT change any logic, just extract components. Each extracted component gets its own file under the page's directory (e.g., frontend/app/api-keys/components/).

AFTER: Run npx tsc --noEmit in frontend/ — 0 new errors. Verify UI still works by checking imports.

tasks.description: GET /v1/invoices/:id/pdf — generate and return a PDF invoice for download
tasks.context:
User-facing feature: agents and their customers need to download PDF invoices for accounting.

IMPLEMENTATION:
  Use the 'pdfkit' npm package (check if already installed: grep pdfkit backend/package.json).
  If not installed: add to dependencies — but DO NOT run npm install; add to package.json manually and note that the server will install on next deploy.

FILE: backend/src/routes/invoices.ts — add after existing routes:
  GET /:id/pdf
  1. Fetch invoice by id from Prisma (include customer, seller info)
  2. If not found: return 404
  3. Generate PDF with pdfkit:
     - Header: 'INVOICE' title + Invoica branding
     - Invoice details: invoiceNumber, date, dueDate, status
     - Seller info: agentId / sellerName
     - Customer info: customerName, customerEmail
     - Line items: serviceDescription, subtotal, tax (if any), total
     - Payment details: paymentAddress, chain, txHash (if settled)
     - Footer: 'Generated by Invoica — invoica.ai'
  4. Set headers: Content-Type: application/pdf, Content-Disposition: attachment; filename='INV-xxx.pdf'
  5. Pipe PDF stream to response

KEEP IT SIMPLE: flat single-page PDF, no complex layout. Max 80 lines for the route handler.

TEST FILE: backend/src/routes/__tests__/invoice-pdf.test.ts
  - 200-returns-pdf-content-type
  - 200-returns-content-disposition
  - 404-not-found
  - 500-db-error

AFTER: npx tsc --noEmit — 0 new errors.

tasks.description: GET /v1/agents/leaderboard — top agents ranked by total settled revenue with reputation scores
tasks.context:
Foundation endpoint for the Agent Marketplace (Priority 2 in roadmap). Surfaces top-performing agents ranked by revenue.

RESPONSE shape:
  {
    agents: [
      {
        rank: number;
        agentId: string;
        totalRevenue: number;      // sum of SETTLED+COMPLETED invoice amounts
        invoiceCount: number;
        settlementRate: number;    // 0–1
        reputationScore: number;   // 0–100 (same computation as REPUTATION-001)
        reputationTier: string;    // bronze/silver/gold/platinum
      }
    ];
    total: number;                  // total number of agents
    computedAt: string;
  }

QUERY PARAMS:
  ?limit=N (default 10, max 50)
  ?tier=gold|platinum (optional filter by reputation tier)

IMPLEMENTATION:
  FILE: backend/src/routes/agents.ts — add GET /leaderboard before /:agentId routes
  1. Query: GROUP BY agentId, SUM amount WHERE status IN [SETTLED, COMPLETED], ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC, LIMIT N
  2. For each agent, compute reputation score inline (reuse same formula as REPUTATION-001 — extract to a shared helper if possible)
  3. Return ranked array

NOTE: If REPUTATION-001 is not yet merged, implement the score computation inline (don't create a dependency blocker). The inline formula is described in REPUTATION-001 context above.

TESTS: backend/src/routes/__tests__/agent-leaderboard.test.ts
  - 200-returns-agents-array
  - 200-has-rank-field
  - 200-has-reputation-score
  - 200-respects-limit
  - 200-empty-when-no-agents
  - 500-db-error

AFTER: npx tsc --noEmit — 0 new errors.

tasks.description: Fix backend-wrapper.sh phantom restart loop — replace lsof with ss for port 3001 check
tasks.context:
SYMPTOM: PM2 shows 769+ backend restarts. Root cause: `lsof -ti :3001` silently returns nothing when run as the `invoica` user (no permission to inspect other processes' FDs on this Linux kernel config). This causes PORT_FREE=true immediately on every restart attempt, so the new ts-node instance fails with 'Port 3001 is already in use' — triggering another PM2 restart. The actual ts-node process holds port 3001 and serves traffic correctly (71257s+ uptime), but PM2 keeps restarting the wrapper.

FILE TO EDIT: /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/backend-wrapper.sh (server-side) AND backend-wrapper.sh (local repo)

CHANGE 1 — Port check loop (lines 43-49):
  CURRENT:
    for i in $(seq 1 30); do
      if ! lsof -ti :3001 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        PORT_FREE=true
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done

  REPLACE WITH:
    for i in $(seq 1 30); do
      if ! ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep -q ':3001 '; then
        PORT_FREE=true
        break
      fi
      sleep 1
    done

CHANGE 2 — Force-kill fallback (line 53):
  CURRENT:
    lsof -ti :3001 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

  REPLACE WITH:
    ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | awk '/:3001 /{print $NF}' | grep -oP 'pid=\K[0-9]+' | xargs -r kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

EXACT OLD_STRING for Change 1:
  for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    if ! lsof -ti :3001 >/dev/null 2>&1; then

EXACT OLD_STRING for Change 2:
    lsof -ti :3001 | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null || true

STEPS:
  1. Edit backend-wrapper.sh locally (both changes above)
  2. Verify the file is correct: cat backend-wrapper.sh
  3. git add backend-wrapper.sh && git commit -m 'fix: backend-wrapper.sh — replace lsof with ss for port check (fixes phantom restart loop)' && git push
  4. Wait for git-autodeploy to pull (up to 5 min), then: pm2 restart backend
  5. Watch: pm2 logs backend --lines 20 --nostream
     Expected: '[backend-wrapper] Lock acquired', then ts-node starts — NO 'Port 3001 is already in use'
  6. Write report to reports/cto/fix-010-backend-wrapper-2026-03-19.md:
     - What was broken and why
     - Changes made
     - PM2 restart count before/after
     - Confirmation that restart loop is resolved

tasks.description: Fix CEO bot wallet alert spam — reduce reminder frequency from 4h to 24h
tasks.context:
SYMPTOM: Tarek receives 5+ Telegram wallet low-balance alerts per day per wallet, all showing $0.00 balance. All agent wallets are unfunded (no active USDC spending yet). The alerts are technically accurate but spammy — ALERT_REMINDER_MS is set to 4h so each of 5 wallets fires 6 times/day = 30 alerts/day.

FILE: backend/src/telegram/ceoBot.ts

FIX: Change ALERT_REMINDER_MS from 4h to 24h:
  Find line: `const ALERT_REMINDER_MS = 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // re-alert every 4h (was 30min)`
  Replace with: `const ALERT_REMINDER_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // re-alert every 24h`

AFTER CHANGE:
  git add backend/src/telegram/ceoBot.ts && git commit -m 'fix: reduce wallet alert spam from every 4h to every 24h' && git push

NOTE: git-autodeploy only restarts ceo-ai-bot when run-ceo-bot.ts changes, NOT when ceoBot.ts changes (FIX-013 will fix this). After pushing, the devops agent should also update scripts/git-autodeploy.sh to watch ceoBot.ts (see FIX-013 context). If FIX-013 is a separate task, the devops agent here should just make the code change and push — the autodeploy fix will be a follow-up.

tasks.description: Fix git-autodeploy.sh to restart ceo-ai-bot when ceoBot.ts changes (not just run-ceo-bot.ts)
tasks.context:
SYMPTOM: Changes to backend/src/telegram/ceoBot.ts (the main bot logic) are never picked up by git-autodeploy. The autodeploy only restarts ceo-ai-bot when scripts/run-ceo-bot.ts changes. This caused the haiku model fix to sit undeployed for 3+ days until manual restart.

FILE: scripts/git-autodeploy.sh

FIX: Update the ceo-ai-bot restart condition to also trigger on ceoBot.ts changes.

Find this block:
  # CEO AI bot (standalone process — replaces the old telegram-bot PM2 entry)
  if echo "$CHANGED" | grep -q "^scripts/run-ceo-bot\.ts$"; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] [AutoDeploy] run-ceo-bot.ts changed → restarting ceo-ai-bot"
    pm2 restart ceo-ai-bot
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] [AutoDeploy] ceo-ai-bot restarted"
  fi

Replace with:
  # CEO AI bot — restart when entry point OR main bot logic changes
  if echo "$CHANGED" | grep -qE "^scripts/run-ceo-bot\.ts$|^backend/src/telegram/ceoBot\.ts$"; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] [AutoDeploy] ceo-ai-bot source changed → restarting ceo-ai-bot"
    pm2 restart ceo-ai-bot
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] [AutoDeploy] ceo-ai-bot restarted"
  fi

AFTER CHANGE:
  git add scripts/git-autodeploy.sh && git commit -m 'fix: git-autodeploy — restart ceo-ai-bot on ceoBot.ts changes too' && git push

tasks.description: Build site health monitor — daily script that checks invoica.ai and app.invoica.ai for HTTP errors, JS failures, and API smoke tests, writes JSON report, triggers CTO alert on failure
tasks.context:
There is no frontend/site health monitoring. A Vercel deploy error or JS crash could silently break onboarding for days during the free beta. This task creates a lightweight health monitor.

FILE: scripts/run-health-check.ts (new file, max 150 lines)

CHECKS TO PERFORM:
  1. Landing page: GET https://invoica.ai — expect HTTP 200, body contains 'Invoica' (anti-blank-page check)
  2. App dashboard: GET https://app.invoica.ai — expect HTTP 200, body contains '<html'
  3. Backend API smoke tests (3 endpoints):
     - GET https://api.invoica.ai/health (or /v1/health if /health not registered) — expect 200
     - GET https://api.invoica.ai/v1/invoices — expect 200 or 401 (NOT 500)
     - GET https://api.invoica.ai/v1/agents/leaderboard — expect 200 or 404 (NOT 500)
  4. Response time check: each request must complete in < 5000ms

IMPLEMENTATION:
  - Use node's built-in https.get (no extra deps) — or fetch if Node 18+
  - For each check, record: url, status, ok (bool), responseMs, error (string|null)
  - Overall: healthScore = (passing checks / total checks) * 100
  - Write report to /home/invoica/memory/health-check-latest.json AND reports/health/YYYY-MM-DD.json

REPORT SHAPE:
  {
    checkedAt: string;   // ISO
    healthScore: number; // 0–100
    allOk: boolean;
    checks: [
      { name: string; url: string; status: number|null; ok: boolean; responseMs: number; error: string|null }
    ];
  }

ALERT LOGIC:
  If allOk === false AND process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID are set:
    Send a Telegram message via the bot: '⚠️ Invoica health check FAILED: [list failed checks]'
    Use the existing ceo-ai-bot credentials (check scripts/ for how Telegram is called elsewhere)

PM2 REGISTRATION:
  In ecosystem.config.js — add to Invoica section:
    {
      name: 'health-monitor',
      script: 'npx ts-node scripts/run-health-check.ts',
      cron_restart: '0 8 * * *',  // daily at 08:00 UTC
      autorestart: false,
      watch: false,
    }

CTO INTEGRATION:
  The CTO daily scan (scripts/run-cto-daily-scan.ts) should read /home/invoica/memory/health-check-latest.json.
  If the file is <24h old and allOk === false, the CTO should include a 'Health Monitor Alert' section in its daily report and propose a fix sprint.
  Check if run-cto-daily-scan.ts already reads from /home/invoica/memory/ — if so, add a health check read there.
  If not, add a SEPARATE step at the END of run-cto-daily-scan.ts: read health-check-latest.json and append findings to the CTO report.

AFTER WRITING: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v node_modules — 0 new errors.
